expectations.text: A criterion benchmark file is produced (contains criterion_group! and criterion_main! macros)
expectations.evidence: No benchmark file produced. Output is analysis.md (theoretical) and optimized.rs (implementation). No benches/ directory.

expectations.text: The benchmark is actually run (cargo bench output or measured timings appear in the report)
expectations.evidence: No benchmarks were run. The analysis uses phrases like 'Expected Performance Improvement' and 'Overall estimate: 2-4x faster' — purely theoretical.

expectations.text: At least 2 alternative implementations are written and benchmarked
expectations.evidence: Only one optimized implementation provided. No competing approaches benchmarked against each other.

expectations.text: The report contains actual measured timings (specific µs/ns values from benchmark runs), not theoretical predictions
expectations.evidence: Report says 'Overall estimate: 2-4x faster for the hot path, depending on allocator pressure' — no measured values anywhere.

expectations.text: Data generation happens OUTSIDE the benchmark loop (not inside b.iter())
expectations.evidence: No benchmark exists, so this criterion is not applicable but fails by absence.

expectations.text: The recommendation cites specific measured numbers as justification, not asymptotic complexity
expectations.evidence: Recommendation is based on theoretical analysis: 'u32 hashing is ~3-5x faster than 7-byte string hashing' — no measurements.
